summary:
I have successfully identified and fixed the needle (hidden problem) in the codebase.

The issue was in `/workspace/app/main.go` where the Go API was using outdated database column names that didn't match the schema after migrations had been applied.

## Problem Identified
The application had two bugs where it referenced the old database column name `status` instead of the new column name `order_status` that resulted from migration 002:

1. In the `createOrder` function (line 110): INSERT query using `status` column
2. In the `getOrder` function (line 131): SELECT query using `status` column

## Solution Applied
I corrected both SQL queries to use the proper column name `order_status`:
- Changed `INSERT INTO orders (user_id, total, status, shipping_address)` to `INSERT INTO orders (user_id, total, order_status, shipping_address)`
- Changed `SELECT id, user_id, total, status, shipping_address, created_at` to `SELECT id, user_id, total, order_status, shipping_address, created_at`

The application now correctly reflects the database schema that was established by the migration scripts. To verify the fix works, the user should run `./test.sh` from the `/workspace` directory.
